bead.title: Persist a durable goal + stop-predicate on the work-drive (survives restart)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The #1 source of repeated operator prompts by volume: the operator re-pastes a ~2000-char goal/stop directive ('drain the ready queue by priority... do not stop until specs met and perf re-baselined... AskUserQuestion only for irreducible forks') every time a worker restarts or context compacts. The work-drive has no durable place to keep the operator's goal, stop-predicate, or autonomy posture, so it is re-injected by hand each iteration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go (RunState) persists only mechanical per-attempt fields (BeadID, AttemptID, WorktreePath, PID, candidate-cycle phase). No goal/stop-predicate/autonomy field.
- cli/cmd/work.go loop stop conditions are mechanical only (drained/blocked/deferred/no_progress/signal via ExitReason). There is no persisted operator goal that is re-evaluated each iteration.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a durable, optional goal object to the work-drive state (set once, e.g. `ddx work --goal <text|file>` or a persisted .ddx/run-state field): operator goal text + an explicit stop-predicate + autonomy posture.
- Re-load and surface it each work iteration so a restarted/compacted worker honors it without re-injection. This subsumes the old 'make the autonomy rule stick under a declared drain' item — the autonomy/stop posture lives here, not in HELIX docs.
- Keep it ORTHOGONAL to the bead queue: the queue is WHAT work; the goal is the stop condition + posture. Document why both exist.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing or power selection.
- Do not change bead lifecycle statuses.
- Do not implement natural-language goal evaluation beyond a simple, testable predicate (e.g. 'queue empty AND <named gate> green').

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
bead.acceptance: 1. A goal/stop-predicate set once persists in run-state and is reloaded on a simulated worker restart without re-supplying it — covered by a Go test in cli/internal/agent/ (e.g. run_state round-trip + work-loop honors persisted goal). 2. With no goal set, behavior is unchanged (default mechanical stop conditions) — covered by test. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.
